rationale: This is the poloidal magnetic flux (ψ) serving as the primary radial coordinate labeling flux surfaces in the equilibrium reconstruction.
reviewer_comment: Correct physical quantity, proper controlled vocabulary, no abbreviations or IDS leakage.

rationale: This is the elongation (κ) of the plasma boundary cross-section, a key shaping parameter defined as the ratio of half-height to minor radius.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity with proper qualifier. Grammar-compliant using 'of_plasma_boundary' modifier.

rationale: This is the major radius (R) coordinate of points along the plasma boundary outline.
reviewer_comment: Uses abbreviation 'r' instead of 'major_radius'. Should be 'major_radius_of_plasma_boundary_outline' or similar.

rationale: This is the reference major radius (R0) at which the vacuum toroidal magnetic field is specified, used for coordinate and normalization definitions.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity and good grammar. Clear and descriptive with proper controlled vocabulary.

rationale: This is the total plasma current (Ip) as measured by the magnetics diagnostic.
reviewer_comment: Correct physical quantity, concise, proper controlled vocabulary, no IDS leakage.

rationale: This is the poloidal magnetic flux measured by a flux loop diagnostic sensor.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity. 'measured' is slightly unusual as a modifier but acceptable for distinguishing diagnostic data.

rationale: This is the major radius (R) coordinate of the poloidal magnetic field probe position.
reviewer_comment: Uses abbreviation 'r' instead of 'major_radius'. Should be 'major_radius_of_poloidal_field_probe_position'.

rationale: This is the physical length of the poloidal magnetic field probe coil, a geometric parameter for sensor characterization.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity, clear grammar. Describes the physical length of the probe coil appropriately.

rationale: This is the diffusion coefficient for electron energy transport in the radial direction.
reviewer_comment: Correct physical quantity, proper controlled vocabulary, clean grammar.

rationale: This is the radial electron particle flux across flux surfaces in the core transport model.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity, concise, proper grammar and controlled vocabulary.

rationale: This is the normalized toroidal flux coordinate (ρ_tor_norm) used as the radial grid for core transport profiles.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity, proper grammar. Accurately describes the dimensionless rho_tor_norm coordinate.

rationale: This is the reference major radius (R0) at which the vacuum toroidal magnetic field is specified, used for toroidal flux coordinate definitions.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity, good grammar. Identical to equilibrium entry, consistent naming.

rationale: This is the energy of the neutral beam injection unit, characterizing the injected particle kinetic energy.
reviewer_comment: Correct physical quantity, clean controlled vocabulary, no IDS leakage.

rationale: This is the fractional distribution of beam power among energy components of the neutral beam injection unit.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity, proper grammar, no abbreviations or IDS leakage.

rationale: This is the major radius (R) coordinate of the electron cyclotron launcher beam launching position.
reviewer_comment: Uses abbreviation 'ec' and 'r'. Should use full terms like 'major_radius_of_electron_cyclotron_launcher_position'.

rationale: This is the atomic mass number of the neutral beam injection species.
reviewer_comment: Correct physical quantity for mass number in atomic mass units. Clean and concise.

rationale: This is the electron temperature measured by the Thomson scattering diagnostic channel.
reviewer_comment: Correct physical quantity, proper controlled vocabulary, no IDS leakage.

rationale: This is the line-integrated electron density measured along the interferometer channel line of sight.
reviewer_comment: Correct quantity, proper grammar, matches the m^-2 unit for line-integrated density.

rationale: This is the major radius (R) coordinate of the Thomson scattering measurement channel position.
reviewer_comment: Uses abbreviation 'r' and leaks IDS name 'thomson_scattering'. Should be 'major_radius_of_measurement_position'.

rationale: This is the major radius (R) coordinate of the first point of the interferometer channel line of sight.
reviewer_comment: Leaks IDS name 'interferometer', uses abbreviation 'r'. Overly long. Should use 'major_radius_of_line_of_sight_first_point'.
